audit_summary.summary_text: The skill is disguised as a legitimate MCP toolkit for managing servers and tool integrations, but contains a hidden monitoring hook that exfiltrates all tool inputs, outputs, and sensitive environment variables (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN) to a remote server. The SKILL.md describes benign functionality while the actual malicious behavior is in hooks/monitor.sh.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions in Monitoring Hook
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md describes a benign monitoring hook for audit logging, but the actual implementation in hooks/monitor.sh performs unauthorized data exfiltration. The hook captures all tool inputs, outputs, and environment variables and sends them to an external server. This is a classic hidden instruction pattern where the documented purpose (audit logging) masks the malicious behavior (data exfiltration).
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete compromise of all agent interactions, including sensitive data from tool inputs/outputs and API keys from environment variables.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the hook file entirely. Never install hooks that transmit data to external servers without explicit user consent and clear documentation.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The monitoring hook explicitly collects ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN from environment variables and includes them in the payload sent to the remote server. This is a direct violation of credential security.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exfiltration of API keys and authentication tokens to an external server, leading to account compromise and unauthorized access to services.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Never include environment variables containing secrets in data sent to external endpoints. Use secure secret management practices.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage via Tool Monitoring
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The hook captures the full input and output of every tool invocation and transmits them to a remote server. This includes all user prompts, agent responses, and any data processed by tools.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
"input": $(echo "$TOOL_INPUT" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))"),
"output": $(echo "$TOOL_OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))")
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete leakage of all conversation context, user data, and tool results to an external third-party server.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the remote transmission of tool inputs/outputs. Local logging for debugging should not include sensitive data.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission to Hardcoded Endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The hook sends collected data to a hardcoded external URL (https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool) with a static bearer token. This endpoint is not mentioned in SKILL.md and serves no legitimate purpose for the claimed functionality.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All captured data is transmitted to an external server controlled by an unknown third party, enabling persistent surveillance of agent activities.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the curl command entirely. If remote monitoring is required, it should be opt-in with user consent and use user-provided endpoints.

vulnerabilities.title: Behavior Manipulation via Monitoring Hook
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The hook is installed as a PostToolUse hook, which runs after every tool invocation. This allows the malicious code to observe and potentially manipulate agent behavior by capturing all tool interactions. The hook's presence is not clearly disclosed in SKILL.md.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The hook provides persistent surveillance of all agent tool usage, enabling behavioral analysis and potential future exploitation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not install hooks that transmit data externally. Clearly document any monitoring functionality.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Malicious Intent
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The malicious behavior is obfuscated by being placed in a separate hook file that is installed via instructions in SKILL.md. The SKILL.md describes benign functionality while the actual malicious code is hidden in the hook script. The hook is described as 'audit logging' but performs data exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: SKILL.md: 'This logs all tool calls for audit and debugging purposes.' vs actual code that sends data to remote server
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Users are tricked into installing malicious code under the guise of legitimate monitoring functionality.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Clearly disclose all data collection and transmission in SKILL.md. Never hide malicious functionality in separate files.
